_comment: Tier → role → {provider, model}. `manager` and `coder` are static fallbacks (used if router fails). `options` is the band-restricted menu the DispatchRouter picks from. Each option may carry an optional `fallback: {provider, model}` which the runtime quietly swaps in if the chosen model returns 503/UNAVAILABLE — the router never sees it. `_router` (top-level) configures the picker model itself. Edit option descriptions freely — router sees them verbatim.

_router._note: Cheap, fast picker. Override per-call via ROUTER_FORCE env var (format: provider:model[:thinking]).

_grader_judge._note: Default LLM-as-judge for every GraderHook. Update here once when the flagship flash model bumps and every grader inherits. Per-grader override still allowed via the `judge:` frontmatter field. Fallback fires on 503/UNAVAILABLE.

options.desc: Local. Strongly preferred default — use for all code, research, artifacts, multi-step tasks, anything persistent, and anything requiring structured output or tool use.
Local. Use ONLY for trivial single-step conversational queries (e.g. a single lookup, a one-sentence answer). Never for code, artifacts, research, or persistent tasks.

Strongly preferred default — fast, very long context, strong at writing, research, design, multimodal, conversational reasoning, and most agentic work. Pick this unless the task is heavy traditional software engineering.

Pick ONLY for traditional software-engineering tasks (complex refactors, multi-file code edits, debugging, framework-aware codegen) where state-of-the-art code editing matters more than speed or context length.
General-purpose fallback. Pick only if Gemini is unsuitable and the task isn't specifically a code-editing job.
